methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: QuantumScape
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

private.name: LanzaTech
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $800M+

data.narrative:
T1 Energy, a publicly traded climate and energy company founded by Tom Einar Jensen, has raised over $1 billion to date. The valuation universe for this stage comprises one public comparable (LNZA) and eight private peers, including QuantumScape, LanzaTech, TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, and Helion. The Climate & Energy sector contains 19 companies at similar maturity levels, though the narrow peer set is constrained.

Peer-median triangulation is not applicable here due to insufficient depth in the comparable universe. The analysis identified only two same-stage peers within Climate & Energy when applying strict filters (same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier), falling short of the three-datapoint minimum required for robust median-based valuation. Expanding to all 19 sector peers would introduce material heterogeneity in business models, capital intensity, and commercialization timelines, rendering an all-sector median unreliable as a standalone method.

The limited peer set and publicly available comparables present a significant constraint on triangulation. Any valuation conclusion would depend heavily on alternative approaches such as discounted cash flow analysis or precedent transaction multiples, neither of which are supported by the current dataset. Key uncertainties include the appropriate revenue or EBITDA multiple for this specific technology subsector and the appropriate discount rate given execution and market adoption risks inherent to frontier energy infrastructure.
